drupalNodeEvent.title: Preventative Healthcare for Rural Communities: A U.S.-China Best Practice Dialogue

field_speakers.processed:
1. Gordon G. LIU, PhD.

  • Dean of PKU Institute for Global Health and Development
  • PKU BOYA Distinguished Professor of Economics
  • MOE Yangtze River Scholar Professor of Economics at PKU National School of Development
  • Director of PKU China Center for Health Economic Research
  • Peking University

2. Yingyao Chen, PhD.

  • Professor of Health Technology Assessment
  • Director of the National Health Commission Key Laboratory of Health Technology Assessment
  • Fudan University

3. Lizheng Shi, PhD., MsPharm, MA

  • Director, Health Systems Analytics Research Center
  • Tulane University

4. Kim D. French, MHSA, CAPPM, FCCP

  • Vice President
  • Lung Programs for Ascension Health System in Chicago

5. Susan Garwood, MD

  • National Physician Director for Pulmonary Disease
  • HCA Healthcare

6. Moderator - Scott Tackett

  • Vice President of Global Market Access, Value & Economics
  • Intuitive
  • Board Member
  • U.S. Heartland China Association

field_description.processed: Join us on January 21st for a special webinar, Preventative Healthcare for Rural Communities, the first of a new series of programs around healthcare collaboration and best practice sharing between the U.S. and China.  A panel of leading public health professionals, healthcare practitioners, and administrators from the U.S. and China will share lessons learned from diverse healthcare systems and discuss innovative strategies for advancing preventative healthcare in under-served communities.
